title: Dog age calculator
intro: Convert your dog’s age to human-equivalent years using an epigenetic formula based on DNA methylation patterns.
error_minimum_age: This formula is based on dogs aged 2 months and older.
result_heading: Your dog is {0} in human-equivalent years
result_traditional: The traditional formula (7 × {0}) would give {1} human years.
result_difference_higher: The epigenetic estimate is {0} years higher than the traditional formula.
result_difference_lower: The epigenetic estimate is {0} years lower than the traditional formula.
result_difference_same: Both formulas give a similar result at this age.
share_photo_label: Add a photo of your dog
share_photo_hint: (optional — it will appear on the share card)
share_native_text: My dog is {0} in human-equivalent years based on epigenetic aging!
card_aria_label: Dog age share card: {0} human-equivalent years based on epigenetic aging
about_formula: This calculator uses the formula human age = 16 × ln(dog age) + 31, derived from a study of DNA methylation changes in Labrador retrievers.
about_limitations: This is a simplified approximation. Aging rates vary by breed and size—smaller dogs generally live longer than larger breeds. The formula is most accurate for dogs over one year old.
about_citation: Based on: Wang T et al. (2020) “Quantitative translation of dog-to-human aging by conserved remodeling of the DNA methylome.” Cell Systems 11(2), 176–185.
